html:
In the EU's capital adequacy directive, requirements are placed on the activities of banks and credit market companies (credit institutions), very large securities firms, and certain holding companies.

The proposal includes draft amendments to the laws necessary to implement the changes made to the capital adequacy directive within the framework of the so-called banking package. The proposals include the following:

  • A licensing requirement is introduced for companies from countries outside the European Economic Area that wish to provide central bank-related services from a branch in Sweden.
  • The suitability requirements that apply to the management of credit institutions will apply to very large securities firms and certain holding companies.
  • A requirement is introduced for certain companies to pre-notify information about new management personnel.
  • A requirement is introduced for companies to assess the suitability of certain individuals in leading positions.
  • Application procedures are introduced for certain transactions.
  • A waiting period is introduced for certain employees and contractors at the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority.

The amendments to the law are proposed to enter into force on July 1, 2026, except for the amendments regarding a special licensing requirement and special supervisory provisions for companies from countries outside the European Economic Area that wish to provide central bank-related services from a branch in Sweden, which will enter into force on January 11, 2027.

Proposal for a Parliamentary Decision

Government Proposal:

  1. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law regarding restrictions for certain officials in the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority (Finansinspektionen) when transitioning to other employment.
  2. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Social Security Code.
  3. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Credit Reporting Act (1973:1173).
  4. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Savings Bank Act (1987:619).
  5. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (1991:980) on trading in financial instruments.
  6. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (1992:160) on foreign branches, etc.
  7. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Pawnshop Act (1995:1000).
  8. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (1995:1571) on deposit guarantee.
  9. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (1996:1006) on certain financial activities.
  10. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2000:35) on changing the accounting currency in financial companies.
  11. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2004:297) on banking and financial services.
  12. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2004:297) on banking and financial services.
  13. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2007:528) on the securities market.
  14. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Public Access and Secrecy Act (2009:400).
  15. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2010:751) on payment services.
  16. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2011:755) on electronic money.
  17. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2013:561) on managers of alternative investment funds.
  18. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2014:966) on capital buffers.
  19. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2014:968) on special supervision of credit institutions and securities companies.
  20. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2015:1016) on resolution.
  21. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2016:1306) with supplementary provisions to the EU Market Abuse Regulation.
  22. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2018:677) on the Board for Review of Restrictions for Government Officials and Certain Other Officials.
  23. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Gambling Act (2018:1138).
  24. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2022:1568) on the Swedish National Bank.
  25. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2025:587) on state support for investments in new nuclear power.
  26. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2026:000) amending the Act (2004:297) on banking and financial services.
  27. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for a law amending the Act (2026:000) amending the Act (2014:968) on special supervision of credit institutions and securities companies.
